CREATE TABLE geometries (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, geo GEOMETRY);
INSERT INTO geometries (id, geo) VALUES (1, st_point(0, 51));
INSERT INTO geometries (id, geo) VALUES (2, st_point(0, 52));
INSERT INTO geometries (id, geo) VALUES (3, st_point(1, 51));
SELECT st_intersects(geo, st_point(0, 52), 100) FROM geometries WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, st_intersects(geo, st_point(0, 51), 100) AS intersection FROM geometries;
SELECT a.id, b.id, st_intersects(a.geo, b.geo, 5) FROM geometries a JOIN geometries b ON a.id != b.id;
WITH geom1 AS (SELECT geo FROM geometries WHERE id = 1), geom2 AS (SELECT geo FROM geometries WHERE id = 2) SELECT st_intersects((SELECT geo FROM geom1), (SELECT geo FROM geom2), 50) AS intersects;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM geometries WHERE st_intersects(geo, st_polygon('((0 50, 1 50, 1 51, 0 51, 0 50))'), 0) = TRUE;
SELECT id FROM geometries WHERE st_intersects(geo, st_buffer(st_point(0, 51), 1), 0) = TRUE;
SELECT ?c, (bif:st_intersects (?geo, bif:st_point (0, 52), 100)) FROM geometries WHERE ?m geo:geometry ?geo GROUP BY ?c ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 10;
